title: DSDS Component Definitions
description: Defines the component document. A component document holds the identity of a reusable UI element. The `metadata` object carries optional metadata fields: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. Typed entries in the `documentBlocks` array carry structured docs: the component-scoped kinds (imports, anatomy, api, variants, states, design-specifications) plus the general kinds (guidelines, useCases, accessibility, content, sections).

component.description: A single reusable UI component. The top level holds identity: `identifier`, `name`, and `description`. The `metadata` object holds optional metadata fields — status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links. The `documentBlocks` array holds structured docs as typed entries: imports, anatomy, api, variants, states, design-specifications, plus the general kinds.

kind.description: Identifies this entity as a component.

identifier.description: Machine-readable identifier (e.g., 'button', 'dialog', 'form-field', 'data-table'). MUST be unique within its entity group.

name.description: Display name shown in docs (e.g., 'Button', 'Dialog', 'Form Field', 'Data Table').

description.description: What this component is, what it does, and its role in the design system. CommonMark supported.

metadata.description: Optional metadata fields, keyed by name: status, since, lastUpdated, category, tags, aliases, summary, thumbnail, preview, extends, and links.

documentBlocks.description: All structured docs for this component, as an ordered array of typed document block objects. Accepts the component-specific kinds (imports, anatomy, api, variants, states, design-specifications) plus the general kinds (guidelines, useCases, accessibility, content, sections). The `kind` property on each entry sets its shape. Order matters: tools SHOULD preserve it for display.

agentDocumentBlocks.description: Structured documentation for agent (AI/LLM) consumption only — a partitioned space accepting the same document block kinds as `documentBlocks`. Use it for guidance that would be noise for human readers: generation constraints, disambiguation between confusable entities, machine-checkable rules with evidence. Tools MUST NOT render these blocks in human-facing documentation. Agents SHOULD read both arrays — human-facing blocks first, then these.
